ip_outcome_note: Card name makes Son Goku explicit. 'Finishing Spirit Bomb' references the climactic technique from Dragon Ball Z: Super Android 13! (1992) where Goku absorbed a Spirit Bomb into his Super Saiyan form and destroyed Super Android 13 by punching through his core. Orange gi and golden SSJ aura are canonical Goku visual identifiers.

trivia_bullets.text: Cross Worlds (DBS-B03) is the third main DBS booster set, released 2018. Its thematic anchor is the Dragon Ball Z: Super Android 13! film (1992); Android 13 is the set's headline leader (BT3-056), with green centered on his control strategy. BT3-057 is the climactic narrative counter in that same green column.
sources: WebSearch: Cross Worlds strategy page DBS official

trivia_bullets.text: The 'Finishing Spirit Bomb' refers to the decisive technique from Dragon Ball Z: Super Android 13! (1992): Goku gathered a Spirit Bomb while allies held Super 13 at bay, then absorbed the bomb's energy into his Super Saiyan form and punched through Super 13's core — a unique combination not replicable under standard Spirit Bomb technique rules.
sources: Wikipedia: Dragon Ball Z: Super Android 13!

trivia_bullets.text: EX-Evolve: discard 1 card to play on top of any Green Son Goku with energy cost 5+. When attacking: combo with up to 3 green Battle Cards from Drop Area and draw 1 card. Conditional: if opponent's Son Goku or cost-6-or-less Battle Card leaves field via your skills, gains 'opponent places damage to Drop Area' for the turn. Power: 25000.

Cross Worlds was the first DBS set to establish EX-Evolve as a core mechanic, allowing evolution chains by playing on top of existing units rather than paying full cost — a structural design shift from BT1 and BT2's more straightforward play patterns.
sources: WebSearch: DBS official Cross Worlds strategy notes
